---
title: "Bayesian Inference from Scratch: Coding Your Own Sampling Algorithm"
subtitle: "Bayesian Statistics"
author: "Florian Metwaly"
institute: "University of Amsterdam"
date: today
date-format: "MMMM YYYY"

format:
  uva-revealjs:
    footer: "Bayesian Statistics — Florian Metwaly | University of Amsterdam"

execute:
  echo: true
  eval: false
  warning: false
  message: false

editor: source
---

## Posterior Distribution

- Our **updated belief** about the parameters $\theta$ after seeing the data $y$

$$
\underbrace{P(\theta \mid y)}_{\text{posterior}} =
\frac{\overbrace{P(y \mid \theta)}^{\text{likelihood}}\ \overbrace{P(\theta)}^{\text{prior}}}{\underbrace{P(y)}_{\text{evidence}}}
$$

- **Likelihood** $P(y \mid \theta)$: how well parameters $\theta$ explain the data
- **Prior** $P(\theta)$: what we believed about $\theta$ *before* seeing data
- **Evidence** $P(y)$: normalising constant — makes the posterior integrate to 1


## We don't need the evidence

- $P(y) = \int P(y \mid \theta)P(\theta)\, d\theta$ is often intractable
- $P(y)$ it's just a constant
- So we can work with the **unnormalised** posterior:

$$
P(\theta \mid y) \propto P(y \mid \theta)\, P(\theta)
$$

- Posterior $\propto$ Likelihood $\times$ Prior
- MCMC (Gibbs, MH, HMC) only needs this proportional form

## Conjugate Priors

- A prior is **conjugate** to a likelihood if the **posterior is in the same family** as the prior
- Pair of prior and Likelihood where we know the shape of the posterior
- Updating is just changing the parameters
- No integration needed
- Properties of Gibbs sampling!


## MCMC Sampling

- Imagine we want to sample the parameters $\theta_1$ and $\theta_2$
- Markov chain:
  - $(\theta_1^{(0)}, \theta_2^{(0)}), (\theta_1^{(1)}, \theta_2^{(1)}), (\theta_1^{(2)}, \theta_2^{(2)}), \dots, (\theta_1^{(k)}, \theta_2^{(k)})$
- Conditional distribution of $(\theta_1^{(i)}, \theta_2^{(i)})$ given *all previous samples* only depends on $(\theta_1^{(i-1)}, \theta_2^{(i-1)})$
- Therefore $(\theta_1^{(0)}, \theta_2^{(0)}), (\theta_1^{(1)}, \theta_2^{(1)}), (\theta_1^{(2)}, \theta_2^{(2)}), \dots, (\theta_1^{(k)}, \theta_2^{(k)})$ are not IID


## Gibbs Sampling

- A special case of Metropolis-Hastings 
  - but with **no accept/reject step**
- Idea: update **one parameter at a time**, holding the others fixed
- Each update samples from the **full conditional** posterior:
  - With conjugate priors each full conditional is a **known distribution** $\rightarrow$ we can sample it directly


## Joint and Conditional Posterior

- Joint posterior for $\theta_1$ and $\theta_2$
$$
P(\theta_1, \theta_2 \mid y) \propto P(y \mid \theta_1, \theta_2)P(\theta_1, \theta_2)
$$

Instead we sample the conditional posterior:
$$
P(\theta_1 \mid \theta_2, y) \propto P(y \mid \theta_1, \theta_2)P(\theta_1 \mid \theta_2)
$$

$$
P(\theta_2 \mid \theta_1, y) \propto P(y \mid \theta_1, \theta_2)P(\theta_2 \mid \theta_1)
$$


##

0. Set starting values $(\theta_1^{(0)}, \theta_2^{(0)})$

::::: columns
::: {.column width="50%"}
1. Iteration
    - Sample $\theta_1^{(1)} \sim P(\theta_1 \mid \theta_2^{(0)})$
    - Current state: $(\theta_1^{(1)}, \theta_2^{(0)})$
    - Sample $\theta_2^{(1)} \sim P(\theta_2 \mid \theta_1^{(1)})$
    - Current state: $(\theta_1^{(1)}, \theta_2^{(1)})$

:::

::: {.column width="50%"}
2. Iteration
    - Sample $\theta_1^{(2)} \sim P(\theta_1 \mid \theta_2^{(1)})$
    - Current state: $(\theta_1^{(2)}, \theta_2^{(1)})$
    - Sample $\theta_2^{(2)} \sim P(\theta_2 \mid \theta_1^{(2)})$
    - Current state: $(\theta_1^{(2)}, \theta_2^{(2)})$
    
:::
:::::

Repeat for as many iterations as you set


## STAN

- Probabilistic programming language
- Uses Hamiltonian Monte Carlos
  - Also a specific case of MH algorithm
- Doesn't run on its own, needs an interface in e.g. `R`
- In the assignment: STAN model as comparison

## Installation

- Install toolchain
  - [RTools](https://cran.r-project.org/bin/windows/Rtools/) (Windows)
  - [Xcode](https://developer.apple.com/xcode/) (MacOS)
  
```{R}
install.packages("cmdstanr", repos = c("https://mc-stan.org/r-packages/", getOption("repos")))
cmdstanr::check_cmdstan_toolchain(fix = TRUE)
cmdstanr::install_cmdstan(cores = 2)
```

- Troubleshooting: Ask me or visit Getting stared with [CmdStanR](https://mc-stan.org/cmdstanr/articles/cmdstanr.html)




##  {.center-text}

**Thank you!**

f.j.metwaly\@uva.nl
